comments: From a collection of 274 tapes mixing John Peel shows, Friday Rock Shows and Alan Freeman Saturday Shows from 1978 to 1983. The Derby Box was acquired by the John Peel Yahoo Group.

track_listing.details: LP-The Last Record Album; Warner Brothers

transcript.text: ...275 and 285, also on stereo VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Hi there, this is Tommy Vance. Welcome to the Friday Rock Show.
And tonight, we've got sessions by The Only Ones, their latest session.
One from 1975 by Stretch, who are now defunct.
Classic tracks by The Byrds, Little Feet, Barclay James Harvest, Steve Hillage, Jethro Tull and Uriah Heep.
You can hear cuts, new ones from Bill Nelson's new band, also from The Secret and Firefall.
plus the Friday Night Connection.
Three tracks of pure delight
and the answer to last week's FNC.
Somebody out there is in for a £10 record token.
But this Friday, to get the whole thing underway,
here is Rory Gallagher.
You know something?
He was on earlier on the programme,
right in front of this very microphone.
And I promised him I wouldn't play it at the wrong speed.
And I did.
I'll be banished for Derry.
8 Miles High, a really classic track
that was asked for by Andrew Darby of Odd Acres,
Hockham's Lane, Renhold in Bedfordshire.
Prior to that, you heard Souped Up Ford,
which comes from a 10-inch four-track maxi-single
to be played at 45 RPM.
Sorry I didn't start it that way.
It was a combination of the fact that, well,
it looked like a bit of an LP and my stupidity.
Souped up Ford is by, of course, Rory Gallagher, that ace Irish guitarist.
And now, let's hear something from the only ones.
There's one of the real talents in the United Kingdom.
That's Ron Argent on a track from his new solo album, which is called Moving Home.
It's just come out on MCA, and the track was called Smiling.
Before that, the first of four from the band called The Only Ones, a session by them tonight.
The track that you heard from them was Miles From Nowhere.
Well, I'm a few miles from 17 Brabant Road, Liverpool 17,
but I don't think Michael Bell is.
Mike, this is for you.
That's Stretch, and you can't judge a book.
It's just the first of four ace numbers
that we're going to be playing from a session
that was recorded Friday the 12th of December,
way back in 1975, when Stretch were then together.
Which they ain't now.
Drunk it out of the archives because we got a really nice letter
from a gentleman by the name of Kevin Daines,
who lives at Salford Lodge, 27 Avenue Road, Malvern, in Worcestershire.
Care if you've got taste?
And before that, you heard Long Distance Love, and that was by the band called Little Feet.
For Michael Bell, up there, somewhere in Liverpool.
Bill Nelson's got a new band together.
Beautiful piece of music by Barclay James Harvest.
Came out on the album Octoberon in 1976.
Rock and roll star, it was called, and it was played because Thomas Hill, 39, Longlands Lane, Finderne in Derby, wrote in and said,
Oi, Tom, play it.
Before that, you heard a track from an album
that's not coming out till February the 16th.
The LP is going to be called Sound on Sound.
It's the first LP by Bill Nelson's Red Noise.
The cut you heard was out of touch.
If there's anything you particularly would like to hear,
just write to me, let me know.
Tommy Vance, Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
And we'll dig it out of the archives.
That includes sessions as well, BBC Radio 1 sessions.
For Led Zeppelin fans, let me tell you
that the 1969 session done by them will be broadcast on this show
on Friday, the 2nd of February.
But now, the only ones.
Written by George Harrison.
That was from the album L by Steve Hillich, It's All Too Much.
And it was requested by quite a number of people, including Chris Perry.
He lives at the Barton, I think it's Lawn Hitton,
in Launceston, down there in Cornwall.
Wanted me to play it for the Perry brothers,
and also for all his mates and all the Blundells as well,
whoever they may be.
I also played it for a couple of people who live near the RCA factory up there
that makes the records in Washington, Tyne and Weir, Carroll and Terry.
Before that you heard Flaming Torch by The Only Ones, but now back to Stretch.
Great session by Stretch, recorded in 1975.
Shame they are no more.
Kirby's gone off to do his own thing.
In fact, we played a track from his solo album a couple of weeks ago.
And Elmer Gantry's mucking around doing things as well.
Really nice session. That was called Livin' on the Highway.
Here's a nifty new single by The Secret.
The more I hear that, the more I like it.
They've got a really good album out as well.
A new band by the name of The Secret and a single called Night After Night.
It's two minutes to eleven on the Friday Rock Show.
Had a couple of letters this week saying,
what is this bit of music you use on the Friday Night Connection?
Well, I'll jog your memory.
It's the Radio One theme by Van de Graaff Generator.
Time to put a smile on the face of a man by the name of Ian Dart
of 88 Oldway, Chudley, Endeavour.
Ian Dart, you scored a double top because yours was the letter that came out of the bag
with the correct answer to last week's Friday Night Connection.
I'd say about a 97% success rate this week.
Can't fox you up anywhere at all.
Tracks were The King Will Come by Wishbone Ash,
Killer Queen by Queen,
and Princess of the Streets by The Stranglers.
Answer? Royalty.
Now, Mr. Dart has fulfilled his part of the deal
for getting the £10 record voucher.
He's put together three bits of music
with a very strong connection.
See if you can get it together.
15 minutes, Mr Dart.
And that was the Friday Night Connection of Mr Dart.
88 Old Way, Chudley, down there in Devon.
£10 record token coming your way.
Congratulations.
Think you know the answer?
Think you know the Friday Night Connection?
Send your answers to Friday Night Connection.
BBC Radio 1, London, W1A, or WW.

and Hold On. This is for Janet

who likes to hear music in a sleepy
state. He brings it back.
came out in 1969, a classic track.

who's still in a sleepy state, I would imagine.
Here's the finale from The Only Ones.
Put together in the BBC Studios just last December,
that was The Only Ones in their final track tonight,
Nice Session, the track was called Prisoner.
Way back in 75, Stretch put this together, Miss Jones.
Stretch, as they sounded way back in 1975 in the BBC Studios,
that track was called Miss Jones.
And all those cuts are on the album Elastique.
a nice session that must have been to attend.
All right, now, look at yourselves.
And look at yourselves, especially for Alan Roy
up there in Snowbound in Burness.
And that's it for the Friday Rock Show.
Good music, disc jockey's rubbish.
Thanks to our sessions, tonight the only ones in stretch,
both produced by Tony Wilson,
as is the whole programme, excluding the mistakes.
Our studio engineer tonight was John. Thank you, John.
Now, wherever you are, keep warm and enjoy.
I'll see you next Friday at the same time.
Till then, ta-ta.
